_comment: Acceptance test scenarios for the AI Candidate Evaluator. Each entry is a full inbound application email. build_fixtures.py renders these into RFC822 .eml files (with real PDF/DOCX attachments) under ./out/emails/, which ingest_gmail.py then uploads via users.messages.insert. 'expected' describes what the dashboard should show after the first half of the pipeline runs; no follow-up email loop is tested. Scenarios flagged 'needs_user_fixture: true' require the user to drop a file into ./fixtures/ before generation — typically scanned PDFs or other hand-crafted artifacts.

scenarios.title: Strong candidate, complete
scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence (Jane Park)
scenarios.body:
Hi team,

Excited to apply. Resume attached.
GitHub: https://github.com/janepark-ex
Portfolio: https://janepark.dev

Thanks,
Jane

scenarios.title: Borderline candidate, manual review
scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence
scenarios.body:
Hello,

Please find my resume attached. GitHub in resume.
Portfolio: https://marcosilva.work

prerequisite.note: First ingest E46_prior.eml (same sender, no attachment) so dashboard has an incomplete row to flip.

scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence

scenarios.title: Rubric is edited while candidate is in-flight
scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence
scenarios.orchestration_hint: Ingest this email, pause pipeline before score step, mutate settings, resume. build_fixtures only emits the .eml; the test harness drives the pause/mutate.

scenarios.title: Rubric says 'PostgreSQL', resume says 'Postgres' (and vice versa)
scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence

scenarios.title: Resume containing SSN, DOB, and photo
scenarios.subject: Application — Builder in Residence

resume_content._comment: Plain-text resume content, keyed by resume_key. build_fixtures.py lays these out onto PDF/DOCX. Lines prefixed with '##' become headers in rendering. Keep these long enough for the evaluator to extract the full profile; 150–300 words is typical.
resume_content.jane_park_strong:
## Jane Park
Senior Full-Stack Engineer — 9 years
jane.park@example.com  |  San Francisco, CA

## Summary
Shipped 4 production products end-to-end; led a team of 6 at Series B fintech. Deep Python/TypeScript.

## Experience
Staff Engineer, Lattice (2022–present) — Led rewrite of core review pipeline; cut p95 latency 60%.
Senior Engineer, Stripe (2018–2022) — Owned Radar rules engine; shipped 12 features generating $40M ARR.
Engineer, Dropbox (2016–2018) — File sharing infra; on-call lead.

## Skills
Python, TypeScript, React, PostgreSQL, Redis, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, OpenTelemetry.

## Shipped Products
Lattice Reviews v2 (2023, 300k users). Stripe Radar Rules (2020). Dropbox Smart Sync beta.

## Education
BS Computer Science, Carnegie Mellon, 2016.

## Links
https://github.com/janepark-ex  https://janepark.dev

## Dr. Longwind
Academic Software Engineer — 15 years

Full academic CV with publications, grants, teaching, service — designed to span 20+ pages after expansion. The generator should repeat the Publications block to reach 24 pages.

Employment:
Assistant Professor, University (2018–present).
Postdoc, Lab (2016–2018).
PhD Student, University (2011–2016).

Skills: Python, C++, CUDA, distributed systems.

https://github.com/longwind  https://longwind.edu

## Publications (will be repeated by generator)
1. Smith & Longwind, 'On the fluent structure of distributed buffers', OSDI 2019.
2. Longwind et al., 'Scaling stateful services to 10k nodes', SOSP 2020.
3. Longwind, 'A theory of retry budgets', EuroSys 2021.
4. Longwind & Park, 'Practical consistency for edge caches', NSDI 2022.
5. Longwind, 'The myth of linearizability in practice', HotOS 2023.
